title: Rapid Species Identification in Mixed Meat Products via qPCR
author: Sophian, Alfi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Food   fraud     involving     mixed     meat     products     is     a     growing   global   concern  ,   posing     risks     to     consumer     health  ,   religious     compliance  ,   and     trade     integrity  .   Conventional     morphological     or     biochemical     tests   are   often     unreliable     when     meat     is     processed     into     complex     matrices     such   as   sausages  ,   nuggets  ,   or     canned     products  .   Quantitative   PCR (  qPCR  )   enables     rapid  ,   specific  ,   and     sensitive     detection     of     animal     species  ,   even   in   highly     processed     foods  .   This     technology     amplifies     species-specific   DNA   sequences   in real   time  ,   allowing     precise     identification     within     hours  .   Implementing     qPCR-based     species     authentication   in   national     food     safety     systems     improves     supply     chain     transparency  ,   strengthens     regulatory     oversight  ,   and     supports     consumer     trust  .   Standardized     protocols     and     validated     reference     materials   are   crucial     to     ensure     consistent     results     and     facilitate     international     harmonization     of     authenticity   testing   programs  .
